fresh_facts.claim: US President Trump issued threats to strike Iranian power plants and bridges by Tuesday 8 PM Eastern Time unless Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz.
US Central Command confirmed ongoing strikes on Iran are continuing.
The Pentagon rescued a stranded US airman alive after his jet was downed over Iran.
Iran's central military command warned of 'much more devastating and widespread' offensive and retaliatory operations if attacks on civilian targets repeat.
Tehran claimed its intelligence chief was killed by the US.
The Iranian parliament speaker urged Trump to respect Iranian rights and end the 'dangerous game.'
Following US-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026 that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, Iran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, his son, as successor.
Mojtaba Khamenei recently warned adversaries to lose their security amid strikes near Tehran.
Iran named hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, ex-IRGC deputy, as new Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in an Israeli strike.
US President Trump announced a 15-point peace plan delivered through Pakistan encompassing Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran confirmed reviewing a peace plan through 'friendly states' like Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey, though denied direct negotiations with the US.
Trump postponed attacks on Iranian energy sites for five days ending around March 30-31, 2026.

Iranian officials, including Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, insisted no direct talks occurred and demanded an end to fighting on all fronts with security guarantees.
Israel killed an IRGC naval commander on March 26, 2026.
Trump planned to deploy more US Marines to the Middle East by late March 2026.
A new ceasefire proposal was received by the US and Iran for an immediate halt and path to ending the war.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 39 of conflict shows Trump issuing ultimatum threats to strike Iranian power plants and bridges by April 8 unless Strait of Hormuz reopens, while ongoing US strikes continue with recent airman rescue and claimed Iranian intelligence chief killed. Iran's hardline military command counters with threats of 'much more devastating' operations, and a new ceasefire proposal has emerged amid contradictory diplomatic signals.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump issued explicit ultimatum threatening strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges by Tuesday 8 PM Eastern Time unless Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz, marking escalation from the 5-day pause.
